transcription:
The more message you receive, the more confidence
a process have, but there is no certainty.

Reliable delivery: If not <u>all</u> messages are lost, the
message will be eventually delivered.

To send a messages: (reliable delivery)
  - put it in a send buffer
  - After a given timeout send what's in the
  buffer
  - when an ack is received, delete it from
  the buffer

  To receive and deliver a message:
  - Ack the sender
(At least once delivery)

<u>Some messages which change state</u> would
mess up state if duplicated

<u>Idempotent</u> messages can be delivered multiple
times

Exactly-once delivery? Only idempotent messages
— message de-duplication with metadata

Robust design is easier with idempotent messages

when an ack is received, delete it from the buffer
